articles.title: HUGGING FACE AND GCP PARTNERSHIP
articles.summary: Hugging Face has partnered with Google Cloud to give easy access to models and compute for customers of both platforms.

articles.title: NEW GPT-4 TURBO AND EMBEDDING MODELS
articles.summary: OpenAI released a new embeddings model, a better and cheaper GPT-3.5 Turbo, and a less lazy GPT-4 Turbo preview. It has improved pricing and tried to improve the GPT-4 Turbo preview model to prevent the laziness issue in code generation.

articles.title: QDRANT RAISES $28M SERIES A
articles.summary: The vector database company that powers portions of ChatGPT and X's “More like this” has raised money to improve its Rust-based vector store and expand its enterprise offerings.

articles.title: DITTOGYM: LEARNING TO CONTROL SOFT SHAPE-SHIFTING ROBOTS
articles.summary: Soft robotics is a challenge due to the large control space. This work introduces a simulator with various tasks for manipulating soft “ditto” like objects. It contains utilities, visualization, and some strong baselines.

articles.title: SCENE GRAPH GENERATION WITH TRANSFORMERS
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a new method for creating scene graphs, making the process faster and more efficient. Their transformer-based technique focuses on improving the way the model understands and connects different elements in an image, leading to better performance on challenging tasks.

articles.title: SPEECH GENERATION WITH SPEECHGPT-GEN
articles.summary: SpeechGPT-Gen is a new speech synthesis model that efficiently handles the complexities of language and voice characteristics.

articles.title: POWER BI ON MICROSOFT FABRIC: MARKETING OR A REAL SEMANTIC LAYER SOLUTION?
articles.summary: Microsoft is marketing its Fabric Lakehouse architecture as an ideal way to manage Power BI semantic models. This tech talk [12] will examine these claims in light of AtScale’s TPC-DS benchmark [13], measuring Power BI performance on Microsoft Fabric on 100GB, 1TB, and

articles.title: 10TB of data. Tune in for the results ↗
LLM STEER - LIBRARY FOR CONTROLLING GENERATION
articles.summary: LLM Steer is a library for controlling language model generation with sentiment vectors. This means you can embed a concept like “thoughtful” and make the generation more thoughtful. It tends to work better than prompting in general, but more study is needed.

articles.title: ROTATION MANIPULATION IN PYTORCH
articles.summary: RoMa is a library for PyTorch for dealing with tricky rotations. It is reasonably efficient and can be used to help with any project that uses 3D data.

articles.title: EVALUATION BOARD OF MULTI-TURN LLM
articles.summary: AgentBoard is a benchmark designed for multi-turn LLM agents, complemented by an analytical evaluation board for detailed model assessment beyond final success rates.

articles.title: IMPLEMENT A SPARSE MIXTURE OF EXPERTS LANGUAGE MODEL FROM SCRATCH
articles.summary: A walkthrough with code on writing a sparse Mixture of Experts model from scratch in Pytorch. There are diagrams and explanations for each step. The explanation of top-k routing is particularly insightful.

articles.title: AI TODAY AND TRENDS FOR AN AI FUTURE
articles.summary: A survey of experts on how early adopters are using AI today and where AI is going in 2024.

articles.title: BETTER IMAGE SIMILARITY METRIC
articles.summary: Image similarity systems give a score based on how similar two images are. This work improves upon previous methods, primarily by relying on synthetic data and human preferences.

articles.title: 3D MEDICAL IMAGE SEGMENTATION
articles.summary: SegMamba is a model designed for 3D medical image segmentation. It offers a more efficient alternative to the Transformer architecture.

articles.title: SPARKSOCIAL (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Track keywords across social media to convert leads with AI-generated auto-replies.

articles.title: IMPROVING SEMANTIC SEGMENTATION WITH A NEW METHOD
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a Shared Feature Calibration (SFC) method to enhance semantic segmentation.
